supersedes_for_decision_purposes: live_predictions/2026-05-05.json (commit 5474cd6) — stale data; this v2 is the post-rerun snapshot
constitution_note: v1 commit 5474cd6 is NOT being deleted or rewritten — it remains the timestamped record at the moment of call. v2 documents what fresh data shows and is what we trade on going forward today.

lead_with_contradiction.what_changed: 5 days of stale price data; rerun on fresh data dropped the top score from 0.897 (KENNAMET) to 0.179 (KOVAI). Score compression averages -52% across the locked top-5.
lead_with_contradiction.kennamet_high_score_was_fragile: KENNAMET dropped 84% (0.897 -> 0.141) on 4-5 days of feature drift — calibrator's high-confidence band is unstable; treat as confirmation that the 0.897 was bin-edge noise, not signal.

model_calibration_diagnostic.implication: 2x-at-80%-confidence remains structurally unreachable under this model. Fresh top-5 scores are 0.16-0.18 — these are RELATIVE rankings, not '18% calibrated probability of doubling'.

sizing_rule.demoted_kennamet_kirlfer: if already opened from v1, hold at smaller weight (0.5pct each) but do not add

operational_lesson: Per CONSTITUTION.md §1.7: predictions made on stale data must be re-checked against fresh data before action. The v1 was a directional pointer, the v2 is the actionable basket. The infinite hypothesis loop will continue and may produce v3 if a fetcher delivers a KEEP verdict.
